title: Work-Sharing Program - Contact us - Canada.ca
content:
## Tariffs: Special measures
These Special Measures, originally implemented in response to the impact of tariffs, are extended from March 6, 2026, to March 31, 2027 , to ensure continued support for employers and workers facing temporary reductions in business activity.
More information is available under ' Special measures ' on the Work-Sharing Program page.

### Employer Contact Centre
Employers seeking  more information on the Work-Sharing Program may contact the Employer  Contact Centre to find the following information:
- how to register or obtain support for applying online via the Grants and Contributions Online Services online platform
- eligibility criteria
- how to register or obtain support for Data Gateway, or
- how to complete your Utilization Report  (UR)

### Regional Work-Sharing Units
Employers applying for a Work-Sharing agreement by email or mail or making a request to amend their Work-Sharing agreement submit their completed forms preferably by email to their respective regional Work-Sharing Unit:
Atlantic Provinces: esdc.tp-atl-ws-tp.edsc@servicecanada.gc.ca
Quebec: qc-dpmtds-lmsdpb-tp-ws-gd@servicecanada.gc.ca
Ontario: esdc.on.ws-tp.on.edsc@servicecanada.gc.ca
Western Canada and Territories: edsc.wt.ws-tp.esdc@servicecanada.gc.ca

### If you're sending by mail or courier
Work-Sharing Program Service Canada 1 Agar Place Saint John NB  E2L 5G4
Postal service: PO Box 6500 Station Main Don Mills Toronto ON  M3C 0L4
Quebec Processing Centre Service Canada Guy-Favreau Complex 4th Floor West Tower 200 René-Lévesque BLVD W Montreal QC  H2Z 1X4
Western Canada and Territories
Vancouver Programs Unit Service Canada 555 Hastings St W Vancouver BC  V6B 4N6

### Employer Inquiry Unit
Service Canada has created an inquiry unit for employers that are seeking general information related  to the Work-Sharing Program. Enquiries will be responded to by email. Employers may send inquiries to edsc.dgop.tp.rep-res.ws.pob.esdc@servicecanada.gc.ca .

### Employment Insurance Call Centre
For general questions about their  Work-Sharing Employment Insurance benefits and payments, employees can call the Employment Insurance Call Centre .
Please note that for privacy reasons, Service Canada is unable to disclose  information specific to an employee’s file to employers.

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
